framework.name: Dual Holder Binding Mechanisms
framework.description: Two approaches to proving legitimate possession of authority: (1) Cryptographic Key Binding - prove control over private key, device-specific, high security; (2) Claims-based Binding - prove identity through claims like name/DOB, cross-device, long-term use. Financial agents could use cryptographic for high-value transactions, claims-based for persistent authorities across device migrations.
framework.raw_quote: Cryptographic Holder Binding or Cryptographic Key Binding: Ability of the Holder to prove legitimate possession of a Verifiable Credential by proving control over the same private key during the issuance and presentation... Claims-based Holder Binding: Ability of the Holder to prove legitimate possession of a Credential by proving certain claims, e.g., name and date of birth
